name: Via Verde del Val de Zafán – circular from Torre del Compte
name_original: Via Verde del Val de Zafán – circular desde Torre del Compte
region_intro: Touring cycling around Torre Del Compte is characterized by its diverse landscapes within the Matarraña region of Teruel, Aragon, Spain. The area features the Matarraña River, which carves out fertile plains known for fruit orchards, providing pleasant, verdant scenery. To the west, the Beceite Mountains offer more challenging terrain and elevated vistas. A significant feature for cyclists is the Vía Verde Val de Zafán, a converted railway line offering gentler, scenic routes. Best touring cycling routes around Torre Del Compte The most popular touring cycling route is Via Verde del Val de Zafán – circular from La Fresneda, a 17.3 miles (27.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a section of the Vía Verde Val de Zafán, offering a smooth ride through the landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Via Verde del Val de Zafán – circular from Torre del Compte, a moderate 19.9 miles (32.0 km) path. This route also utilizes the Vía Verde, providing accessible cycling with scenic views of the Matarraña region. Local touring cyclists also love the Horta de Sant Joan – Greenway of Terra Alta loop from Antigua Estación de Torre del Compte, a 40.4 miles (65.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 18 minutes. Touring cycling around Torre Del Compte is defined by river valleys, fertile plains, and the converted railway lines of the Vía Verde Val de Zafán. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle greenways to more challenging routes in the foothills of the Beceite Mountains. The routes in Torre Del Compte are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Torre Del Compte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Torre del Compte?
A: There are over 70 touring cycling routes around Torre del Compte, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes over 600 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Torre del Compte?
A: The terrain around Torre del Compte is quite varied. You'll find gentler, scenic routes along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4364668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Val de Zafán&lt;/a&gt;, which is a converted railway line. For those seeking more challenge, the Beceite Mountains provide more demanding terrain and elevated vistas. The region also features fertile plains along the Matarraña River, known for fruit orchards, offering pleasant, verdant scenery.

Q: Are there easy, flat routes suitable for relaxed cycling or families?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde Val de Zafán is ideal for relaxed cycling and families. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955904449/via-verde-del-val-de-zafan-circular-from-torre-del-compte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde del Val de Zafán – circular from Torre del Compte&lt;/a&gt; offer accessible cycling with scenic views. There are 4 easy routes and 26 moderate routes, many of which utilize the greenway.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Along your rides, you can encounter several interesting attractions. The old town of Torre del Compte itself is a Historic-Artistic Site with the Baroque &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Torre_del_Compte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro Mártir&lt;/a&gt; and impressive noble houses. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valderrobres Stone Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357822" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre del Compte Bell Tower&lt;/a&gt;, or even the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2882512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portellada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4364668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Val de Zafán Greenway&lt;/a&gt; itself is a notable feature, often passing through old &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3560752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Railway Tunnels&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Torre del Compte are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955927439/via-verde-del-val-de-zafan-circular-from-la-fresneda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde del Val de Zafán – circular from La Fresneda&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a smooth ride through the landscape.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Torre del Compte?
A: The Matarraña region, including Torre del Compte, is known for its pleasant climate. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for touring cycling, allowing you to enjoy the verdant plains and mountain views without extreme heat. The fertile plains are particularly beautiful when fruit trees are in blossom or bearing fruit.

Q: Where can I park my car when cycling in Torre del Compte?
A: Torre del Compte, being a smaller village, typically offers public parking within the village itself. For routes starting outside the village, such as those along the Vía Verde, there are often designated parking areas near the old railway stations or trailheads. It's advisable to check specific route details for recommended starting points and parking.

Q: Is public transport available to access cycling routes in Torre del Compte?
A: While Torre del Compte is a rural area, some regional bus services connect it to larger towns. However, direct public transport access specifically designed for cyclists to reach trailheads might be limited. It's generally more convenient to arrive by car or cycle directly from your accommodation in the village.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Torre del Compte?
A: The touring cycling routes in Torre del Compte are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from the gentle Vía Verde to the more challenging mountain paths, and the scenic beauty of the Matarraña region.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, the Beceite Mountains provide more demanding terrain. There are 46 difficult routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38225439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horta de Sant Joan – Greenway of Terra Alta loop from Antigua Estación de Torre del Compte&lt;/a&gt;, a longer trail leading through varied and more strenuous terrain.

Q: Are touring cycling routes in Torre del Compte dog-friendly?
A: Many outdoor routes in the Matarraña region are generally dog-friendly, especially those on natural paths and greenways like the Vía Verde. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to ensure they are well-behaved around other cyclists and pedestrians. Always carry water for your dog.

Q: Are there cafes or pubs along the cycling routes for refreshments?
A: While the Vía Verde itself might have limited facilities directly on the path, routes often pass through or near villages like Torre del Compte or La Fresneda. These villages offer cafes, restaurants, and pubs where cyclists can stop for refreshments and meals. It's a good idea to plan your route to include stops in these towns.
tips: The old train station is a good place to stop on the Vía Verde, but no matter how in a hurry it is worth taking a detour to the most beautiful town in the region.
The Matarranya river is what gives name to the region you are in, officially called Matarraña/Matarranya. The Tortosian variant of Catalan is mainly spoken in this region.
Take a look at the old doors and mosaics at the entrance, impressive right?
